title: Race-safe get_or_insert with on_conflict returning
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.Tags.get_or_insert_tag/1` that takes a tag name and returns `{:ok, tag_struct}`. It must be race-safe: two concurrent callers with the same name must both receive the same tag row. Use `Repo.insert/2` with the changeset, `on_conflict: :nothing`, `conflict_target: :slug`, `returning: true`. Handle the case where `:nothing` returns `%Tag{id: nil}` (already existed) by re-querying via slug. Write to `lib/my_app/blog/tags.ex`.
tier_rationale: Knowing that :nothing returns a stub struct with id: nil (vs a full row on successful insert) and handling that case explicitly is a rare + critical pattern.
research_citation: Ecto constraints-and-upserts hexdocs: 'If two posts are submitted at the same time with a similar tag, there is a chance we will check if the tag exists at the same time, leading both submissions to believe there is no such tag'
